constructs: implicit cognition explicit cognition measurement format task structure
claim: Differences in test structure rather than cognitive process generate apparent divergence; equating format reveals much stronger concordance.

constructs: implicit attitude explicit attitude introspection unconscious layers
claim: Implicit and explicit measures diverge because they access distinct attitude representations formed at different developmental periods.

constructs: commitment certainty progress framing motivation inference
claim: Same progress magnitude framed as completed or remaining has opposite motivational effects, with direction determined by whether commitment or progress is psychologically salient.

phenomenon: dissociation between stated and revealed preferences
constructs: introspection preference self-report live interaction mate choice
claim: Stated preferences reflect normative theories about what should matter in partner selection, not introspective insight into what actually determines attraction.

constructs: value importance elaboration resistance attitude strength core values
claim: Linking messages to important values increases resistance through enhanced processing, regardless of whether resulting attitudes express the values themselves.

constructs: commitment certainty progress frame motivation goal adherence inference
claim: Information about accumulated vs. remaining progress shifts from signaling goal importance (uncertain commitment) to signaling goal deficit (certain commitment).
